# THRIVE V — Value and viability

All powers install **Available Inactive**. A dashboard launcher opens a preview only; it never performs an external action.

## PWR-17 — Wellness Service Portfolio & Value-Proposition Architect

**Pillar:** V<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Clarify the approved service portfolio and truthful value propositions while keeping clinical scope and health claims human-governed.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** approved service facts; intended users at aggregate level; alternatives; evidence; capacity; costs; access requirements; service/clinical owners<br>
**Method:** map job-to-be-done and boundaries; distinguish feature, experience, process and health outcome; compare alternatives; test evidence, inclusion, burden and operational fit<br>
**Output:** Service Portfolio Map, value-proposition draft, evidence gaps and governance queue<br>
**Human checkpoint:** service, clinical, finance, legal and operations owners approve claims and portfolio changes<br>
**Prohibited:** no new service creation, clinical scope change, diagnosis, efficacy claim, price, enrollment, eligibility, promise or public offer<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.

## PWR-18 — Budget, Forecast, Marketing-Mix & Value Learning Studio

**Pillar:** V<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Prepare transparent non-transactional scenarios for resources, reach, conversion assumptions, capacity effects and value learning.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** finance-approved aggregate inputs; budget version; period; channel assumptions; outcome definitions; ranges; owner; materiality threshold<br>
**Method:** preserve source/version; separate actual, plan, forecast and estimate; show unit economics as assumptions; run base/upside/downside; include mission, equity, burden and capacity<br>
**Output:** Budget Scenario, mix options, sensitivity table, assumption ledger and finance questions<br>
**Human checkpoint:** finance and accountable leaders validate calculations and decide resources<br>
**Prohibited:** no spend, payment, purchase, journal entry, price, contract, guaranteed ROI, financial statement or transaction<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.

## PWR-19 — Pricing, Promotion & Offer Governance Studio

**Pillar:** V<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Prepare ethical questions and scenarios for price, promotion and offer design without setting a price or executing a transaction.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** approved current price facts; cost and access context; proposed offer; audience aggregate; evidence; terms; equity impacts; legal/finance reviewers<br>
**Method:** separate price from value and clinical benefit; expose assumptions and total terms; test affordability, coercion, urgency, renewal and exclusion risks; prepare alternatives<br>
**Output:** Offer Review, scenario ranges, terms/disclosure questions, equity impacts and decision queue<br>
**Human checkpoint:** finance, legal, compliance, clinical and executive humans approve exact terms<br>
**Prohibited:** no price change, discount activation, coupon, purchase, enrollment, subscription, billing, guarantee, deceptive scarcity or individual offer<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.

## PWR-20 — Vendor, Marketing Technology & Procurement Due-Diligence Studio

**Pillar:** V<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Evaluate proposed tools and vendors across evidence, privacy, accessibility, security, interoperability, cost, lock-in and exit.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** approved requirement; alternatives; vendor claims; data flow; integrations; lifecycle costs; rights; review routes; conflicts<br>
**Method:** separate must-have from preference and claim from evidence; assess implementation burden, data rights, tracking, resilience, support and decommissioning; plan no-purchase option<br>
**Output:** Business Case, requirements matrix, vendor questions, risk/exit register and formal review queue<br>
**Human checkpoint:** procurement, privacy, security, legal, finance, accessibility and operations owners decide<br>
**Prohibited:** no selection, endorsement, signature, purchase, credential, production connection, tracking deployment or claim that due diligence is complete<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.
